gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/358)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/358)
text: بَعَثَ الْوَالِي إِلَى رَجُلٍ مِنْ آلِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ فِي جِنَايَةٍ فَمَرَّ بِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ أَتْبِعُوهُ بِخَاتَمِ عَقِيقٍ فَأُتِيَ بِخَاتَمِ عَقِيقٍ فَلَمْ يَرَ مَكْرُوهاً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Yaqoub Bin Yazeed, from Ibrahim Bin Uqba, from Sayaba Bin Ayoub, from Muhammad Bin Al Fazl, from Abdul Raheem Al Qaseyr who said,

‘The governor sent a summoner to a man from the progeny of Abu Talib<sup>as</sup> regarding a felony, so he passed by Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Follow him with a carnelian ring’. So they brought it (to him) and he did not see any abhorrence’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ya‘qub ibn Yazid from Ibrahim ibn ‘Uqbah from Sayabah ibn Ayyub from Muhammad ibn al-Fadl from ‘Abd al-Rahim al-Qasir who has said the following: “Once, the governor summoned a man of Ale abu Talib in a criminal case, and he passed by abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who said, ‘Follow him with a ring of ‘Aqiq (carnelian)’. ‘Aqiq (carnelian) ring was brought and he did not experience any undesirable matters.’”
